metadata.disclaimer: All information is for research purposes only. Not medical advice. Consult healthcare professionals before use.

categories.name: Healing & Regenerative
categories.description: Accelerate tissue repair, wound healing, reduce inflammation

categories.name: Growth Hormone Secretagogues
categories.description: Stimulate natural GH release from pituitary

categories.name: Fat Loss & Metabolism
categories.description: Target fat loss, increase metabolic rate

categories.name: Hair Growth & Scalp Health
categories.description: Promote hair growth and follicle health

BPC-157.name: BPC-157

chemical.origin: Derived from human gastric juice protein

mechanism.primary: Promotes angiogenesis, accelerates healing through multiple pathways including VEGF and FGF upregulation

legal.notes: Oral supplements sold in some countries; injectable form strictly research-only

BPC-157.safety: Excellent safety profile; high therapeutic index

chemical.origin: Synthetic analog of natural thymosin beta-4

mechanism.primary: Actin-binding peptide that promotes cell migration, angiogenesis, wound healing

administration.half_life: Long (~40 hours serum, tissue retention longer)

female.notes: Smaller individuals may start at 2.5 mg

legal.notes: Not approved for human therapeutic use anywhere

GHK-Cu.name: GHK-Cu

chemical.origin: Natural peptide in human plasma (declines with age)

mechanism.primary: Copper-dependent peptide stimulating collagen synthesis and tissue repair

legal.notes: Widely available in skincare; injectable sold as research chemical

GHK-Cu.safety: Excellent - one of most-studied and safest peptides

Ipamorelin.name: Ipamorelin

mechanism.primary: Selective ghrelin receptor (GHSR) agonist stimulating pulsatile GH release
mechanism.selectivity: Most selective GHRP - minimal cortisol/prolactin increase

benefits: Fat loss and improved body composition

administration.timing: Fasted state - wait 30-60 min before eating

female.notes: 30-50% lower dose vs males, start low

Ipamorelin.safety: Very good - lowest side effect profile among GHRPs

Sermorelin.name: Sermorelin

mechanism.primary: GHRH receptor agonist stimulating natural pulsatile GH release from pituitary

administration.timing: Fasted morning OR pre-bed (nocturnal GH pulse alignment)

stacks.synergy: GHRH + GHRP enhances both pulse frequency and amplitude

legal.US: FDA APPROVED for pediatric GH deficiency (Geref)
legal.adult_use: Off-label use for adult GH deficiency requires prescription

Sermorelin.safety: Excellent - mimics natural physiology, no receptor desensitization
Sermorelin.half_life: ~8-12 minutes (blood); GH pulse effect lasts hours
Sermorelin.note: One of most-studied GH-releasing peptides with extensive safety data

CJC-1295.name: CJC-1295

chemical.modifications: Drug Affinity Complex (DAC) extends half-life by albumin binding

mechanism.primary: Modified GHRH providing sustained GH and IGF-1 elevation
mechanism.warning: With DAC may cause prolonged IGF-1 elevation; some concerns about long-term receptor desensitization

benefits: Sustained elevation of GH and IGF-1
Fat loss and improved body composition
Increased lean muscle mass and strength

long_term_concerns: Potential insulin resistance with DAC variant

stacks.synergy: Gold standard stack - baseline GH + pulse stimulation

legal.notes: DAC variant more restricted due to long half-life concerns

CJC-1295.safety: Good - provides stable GH profile; monitor blood markers
CJC-1295.note: Without DAC usually preferred for better control and lower side effects

Tesamorelin.name: Tesamorelin

chemical.type: GHRH analog with fatty acid modification
chemical.modifications: Hexanoyl group at N-terminus extends half-life

mechanism.primary: GHRH receptor agonist with prolonged half-life (~26 hours), specifically reduces visceral adipose tissue

benefits: Significant reduction in visceral abdominal fat (primary)

legal.US: FDA APPROVED (Egrifta) - prescription only for HIV lipodystrophy
legal.status_note: Most regulated and studied peptide on market; requires prescription

Tesamorelin.safety: Good - most extensive clinical trial data among peptides
Tesamorelin.prescription: Yes - in US for legitimate diagnosis
Tesamorelin.note: Only FDA-approved peptide for fat loss

AOD-9604.name: AOD-9604

chemical.modifications: Tyrosine-to-Phenylalanine substitution at position 177 prevents diabetogenic effects

mechanism.primary: HGH fragment with selective lipolytic activity only
mechanism.vs_HGH: Unlike full HGH - no IGF-1 elevation, no blood sugar disruption, no anabolic effects on muscle

benefits: Improves lipid profile (lowers LDL and triglycerides)
No impact on insulin or blood glucose

legal.notes: Marketed as 'fat loss peptide' in some places

AOD-9604.safety: Very good - lacks metabolic side effects of full growth hormone

chemical.sequence: Amino acids 176-191 of human growth hormone
chemical.notes: Often same compound as AOD-9604 (slight variations possible between batches)

mechanism.primary: HGH fragment mimicking lipolytic region only
mechanism.vs_HGH: No anabolic or glucose effects - pure fat metabolism

Cerebrolysin.name: Cerebrolysin

chemical.components: Mixture of ~20 neurotrophic peptides derived from porcine brain

mechanism.primary: Complex neurotrophic and neuroprotective peptide preparation
actions: Protects against neurotoxicity and oxidative stress
Enhances synaptic plasticity and long-term potentiation
Increases BDNF and NGF activity in CNS

legal.US: Not FDA approved - requires special access program
legal.EU: Approved for stroke/dementia in Austria, Germany, Russia

Cerebrolysin.safety: Good - decades of clinical use in Europe; monitor for allergies
Cerebrolysin.notes: Most studied nootropic peptide with hundreds of clinical trials

chemical.origin: Synthetic analog of nerve growth factor (NGF) fragment

mechanism.primary: Mimics nerve growth factor activity

Semax.name: Semax

mechanism.primary: ACTH fragment with potent nootropic and neuroprotective effects
mechanism.vs_NGF: Does not bind NGF directly - modulates related pathways

legal.RU: Available over-counter as nootropic in Russia

Semax.safety: Very good - decades of use in Russia with excellent safety profile
Semax.notes: One of most popular and well-researched nootropic peptides

chemical.notes: Tuftsin = natural immune peptide, Selank is stabilized version

mechanism.primary: Anxiolytic and nootropic peptide with GABAergic modulation
mechanism.note: Does not cause tolerance or dependency

Epithalon.name: Epithalon

mechanism.primary: Telomerase activation and telomere maintenance
actions: Stimulates telomerase activity in somatic cells
mechanism.caveat: Human telomere elongation data limited; animal studies show lifespan extension

cycle_protocols.protocol_A: 10 days on, 20 days off, 30 days on, 60 days off

legal.RU: Available as dietary supplement in Russia

Epithalon.safety: Very good - Decades of Russian research with excellent safety profile
Epithalon.notes: One of most-studied Russian anti-aging peptides

Thymosin Alpha-1.name: Thymosin Alpha-1

mechanism.primary: Potent immunomodulatory peptide
mechanism.pituitary_independent: Does not stimulate GH or other pituitary hormones

legal.US: FDA APPROVED (Zadaxin) for chronic hepatitis B and C
legal.status: Prescription drug in many countries; available OTC in select markets

Thymosin Alpha-1.safety: Excellent - Very well-tolerated even at high doses

Thymosin Beta-4.name: Thymosin Beta-4

chemical.note: TB-500 is the preferred synthetic, peptide-stable analog; natural Thymosin Beta-4 less stable and not commonly used

mechanism.primary: Same actin-binding and angiogenic mechanisms as TB-500

Thymosin Beta-4.administration: TB-500 is practical; natural version rarely used
Thymosin Beta-4.notes: In practice TB-500 is used - more stable

PT-141.name: PT-141

chemical.origin: Derived from Melanotan II research (Paladin Labs)

mechanism.primary: Selective MC4R agonist acting centrally in hypothalamus
actions: Activates sexual arousal pathways in CNS
mechanism.vs_Melanotan: Does not activate peripheral MC1R - does NOT cause skin tanning

benefits: FDA-approved for Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women
Works for both men (off-label) and women (approved)

administration.timing: As needed 45-60 minutes before sexual activity OR scheduled twice weekly
administration.on_demand_max: 1 dose per 24 hours, max 8 doses per month

management: Take with small meal to reduce nausea

legal.US: FDA APPROVED (Vyleesi) for HSDD in premenopausal women - prescription only

PT-141.safety: Good - nausea main limiting factor; monitor BP

Melanotan II.name: Melanotan II

chemical.origin: Developed by Paladin Labs for skin cancer prevention research

mechanism.primary: Non-selective MC1R/MC3R/MC4R agonist
mechanism.vs_PT141: Peripherally active - causes skin darkening; PT-141 is CNS-selective

tanning.loading: 1 mg daily until desired color (usually 10-15 doses)

pigmentation: Skin darkening (PERMANENT - all skin including moles darkens)
management: Eat before injection to reduce nausea
Start very low dose (0.25-0.5 mg) and titrate slowly

Melanotan II.monitoring_requirements: Monthly skin checks mandatory - Melanotan II increases atypical mole activity and melanoma risk

legal.EU: Banned or restricted in multiple countries
legal.notes: Widely available via grey market but safety concerns

Melanotan II.safety: ⚠ SERIOUS SAFETY CONCERNS - Darkens ALL pigmentation including moles; increases melanoma risk; long-term safety unknown; HAS caused deaths (cardiac events in predisposed)
Melanotan II.caution_level: HIGH - Not recommended for casual use

GHRP-2.name: GHRP-2

chemical.notes: Older compound, less selective than newer options

mechanism.primary: Ghrelin receptor agonist stimulating GH release

stacks.notes: Synergistic but increases cortisol/prolactin more than Ipamorelin stacks

GHRP-2.safety: Moderate - hormonal side effects higher than Ipamorelin
GHRP-2.note: Older GHRP; Ipamorelin generally preferred due to better side effect profile

GHRP-6.name: GHRP-6

mechanism.primary: Very potent ghrelin receptor agonist
mechanism.warning: Least selective GHRP - highest side effect burden

benefits: Most powerful natural GH pulse stimulator
Rapid muscle gain (with adequate caloric intake)

male.max: 200 mcg per dose (higher increases side effects significantly)

female.note: Very sensitive - start at lowest doses

hormonal: May affect sleep if dosed late
management: Post-cycle therapy recommended to normalize cortisol/prolactin
Never dose within 4 hours of bedtime
Monitor prolactin if symptoms (low libido, gyno)

stacks.warning: Powerful but highest cortisol/prolactin risk - only for experienced users
stacks.alternative: Use Ipamorelin instead of GHRP-6 for lower side effects

GHRP-6.safety: Moderate - Strongest side effect profile among GHRPs; caution advised
GHRP-6.caution: Most likely to cause side effects among common GHRPs

Hexarelin.name: Hexarelin

chemical.modifications: 2-methyl-tryptophan (2-Me-Trp) increases potency and resistance to degradation

mechanism.primary: Very potent ghrelin receptor agonist

administration.note: Can desensitize GHRH receptors with prolonged use

caution: Can desensitize GHRH receptors with prolonged continuous use

Hexarelin.safety: Moderate - potent but monitor hormone levels
Hexarelin.note: Rarely used compared to Ipamorelin or CJC due to less favorable side effect:benefit ratio

Semaglutide.name: Semaglutide
Semaglutide.description: GLP-1R agonist; appetite suppression, insulin secretion, glycemic control, weight loss
Semaglutide.tldr: Powerful weight loss and diabetes management via GLP-1 receptor activation
Semaglutide.mechanism: Acts as GLP-1 receptor agonist, slowing gastric emptying, reducing appetite, enhancing insulin secretion in response to meals

Tirzepatide.name: Tirzepatide
Tirzepatide.description: Dual GLP-1R/GIPR agonism; potent weight loss, glycemic control
Tirzepatide.tldr: Most powerful weight loss peptide - dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist
Tirzepatide.mechanism: Activates both GLP-1 and GIP receptors, providing synergistic metabolic benefits

Liraglutide.name: Liraglutide
Liraglutide.description: GLP-1R agonist; diabetes & obesity management, cardiovascular risk reduction
Liraglutide.tldr: Daily GLP-1 agonist for diabetes and weight management
Liraglutide.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist that enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion

Oxytocin.name: Oxytocin
Oxytocin.description: Uterine contraction, lactation initiation, social bonding, anxiety reduction
Oxytocin.tldr: The 'love hormone' - affects social bonding, trust, and sexual function
Oxytocin.mechanism: Binds to oxytocin receptors in brain and body, affecting social behavior, trust, and reproductive functions

MOTS-c.name: MOTS-c
MOTS-c.description: Mitochondrial biogenesis, exercise capacity enhancement, metabolic regulation
MOTS-c.tldr: Mitochondrial-derived peptide that regulates metabolism and exercise response
MOTS-c.mechanism: Enters nucleus to regulate gene expression, improving metabolic flexibility and insulin sensitivity

Follistatin.name: Follistatin
Follistatin.description: Myostatin inhibition, muscle growth, tendon repair
Follistatin.tldr: Powerful myostatin inhibitor - blocks muscle growth limits
Follistatin.mechanism: Binds and neutralizes myostatin, removing the brake on muscle growth

Bremelanotide.name: Bremelanotide
Bremelanotide.description: Hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis stimulation, female/male sexual dysfunction
Bremelanotide.mechanism: Activates melanocortin receptors, particularly MC4R, affecting sexual desire and behavior

Epitalon.name: Epitalon
Epitalon.tldr: Russian anti-aging peptide - increases telomerase activity
Epitalon.mechanism: Stimulates telomerase activity, increases pineal gland function, modulates melatonin
